artifacts: AppImage x86_64 — primary; one fully self-contained file
.deb — convenience twin for Ubuntu/Mint, same payload

floor.os: glibc 2.31+ (Ubuntu 20.04-era, 2020). Build the whole payload against this base so old and new distros both run it.
floor.later: arm64 build from the same manifest

one_package_covers: Wayland + X11 + GNOME + KDE + wlroots in a SINGLE package. The runtime fallback chains (hands/backends/base.py run_chain) pick the working tool live per action — builds are never split by session type, because the session can change under us at the login screen.
tool_provenance: Bundled tools are built in old-glibc containers (packaging/linux/build-tools-container.sh), never on the dev box. TWO tiers by dependency reality: Tier A ydotool+ydotoold on ubuntu:20.04 (glibc 2.31 floor; measured 2.3-2.4) = the GNOME/KDE-Wayland input path that MATTERS; Tier B wtype+grim on ubuntu:22.04 (glibc 2.35; measured 2.34) = wlroots-only rungs needing libxkbcommon>=1.0. All four VERIFIED built 2026-07-12. gnome-screenshot/scrot/xdotool/xdg-open stay system-extra (system-present on the desktops where they apply); static-bundling them needs GTK/libX11 and is deferred, tracked here.

python_extra.note: AT-SPI read_screen/click_element prong; freeze into the bundled python if feasible, else the capability honestly reports false (tri-state doctrine)

ydotool.role: uinput input client — works on X11 AND every Wayland compositor. Built at glibc 2.4 (Tier A, ubuntu:20.04) — runs on ~any Linux of the last 15y.

ydotoold.role: uinput daemon ydotool requires. Ubuntu/Debian apt ships the CLIENT ONLY — no daemon (validated on OC3, 2026-07-11), so without bundling this, GNOME-Wayland input is dead out of the box. Installed as a system service by packaging/linux/firstrun-linux.sh.

wtype.role: wlroots virtual-keyboard typing (fails on GNOME by design — the chain pivots). Built at glibc 2.34 (Tier B, ubuntu:22.04) — wtype needs libxkbcommon>=1.0 absent on the 2.31 floor; fine, wlroots users run modern compositors.

grim.role: wlroots screenshot (fails on GNOME by design — the chain pivots). Tier B, glibc 2.34.

gnome-screenshot.role: GNOME screenshot, works on both X11 and Wayland (validated on OC3 GNOME-Wayland: real 1.37MB PNG). Shipped WITH every GNOME desktop — the priority target has it by default; a static build needs GTK, deferred.

scrot.role: X11 screenshot — present on ~every X11 desktop; grim/gnome-screenshot cover the bundled path.

spectacle.role: KDE screenshot; KDE installs it by default — an extra rung when present

import.role: ImageMagick X11 capture — redundant rung under scrot

xdg-open.role: open_url / open_app fallback — xdg-utils shell script present on every freedesktop desktop; static-bundling deferred (it shells out to desktop-specific openers anyway).

xdotool.role: X11 keyboard+mouse — standard on X11 desktops; ydotool (bundled) covers X11 too, so this is a redundant rung. Static build needs libX11/libXtst, deferred.

gtk-launch.role: desktop-file launcher; present on GTK desktops; xdg-open covers when absent

bash.role: run_shell tool; present on every desktop Linux

portal.role: NOT a binary: the org.freedesktop.portal.RemoteDesktop D-Bus service (xdg-desktop-portal + desktop backend), reached via gi — the SANCTIONED GNOME-Wayland pointer (Phase 1; Mutter ignores ydotool's virtual pointer). Ships with every GNOME/KDE desktop. First use pops a one-time 'allow remote control' grant, remembered via persist_mode=2 + restore token (~/.windytalk/portal_restore_token) — a first-run wizard step (Phase 4).

keyboard_wayland_gnome_kde: ydotool + ydotoold (bundled) — the ONLY keyboard that works here; this is why ydotoold must ship
pointer_wayland_gnome: RemoteDesktop portal (os-builtin) — Mutter ignores ydotool's virtual POINTER (proven 5K + 1080p), so the portal is the only honest pointer; needs the one-time grant
input_wayland_wlroots: portal where the compositor provides RemoteDesktop

first_run_root_step: ONE sudo prompt in the wizard runs packaging/linux/firstrun-linux.sh: installs the uinput udev rule + the windytalk-ydotoold system service, then verifies the socket is alive. Everything else is user-level.
sandboxed_formats_rejected: Flatpak/Snap deliberately NOT offered for the hands payload — their sandboxes block uinput/desktop control, which is the whole product.
